Mode 2b OBE-triage run @ 2026-04-30T16:01:53.136Z.

DISPATCHER TRACE (clean clone refreshed at PRELUDE Step 0a, fresh clone created — no prior session clone existed):
  Mode 0 — WIN onboarding: workspace=71, GitHub=71. NO WORK.
  Mode 1 — Expansion queue: NO ACTIONABLE EXPANSIONS.
  Mode 1b — Predictions (our_verdict null): ALL DONE.
  Mode 2 — Human notes: NO NOTES pending.
  Mode 2b — Attention inbox: dispatcher reports 0 PENDING (status==="pending" filter), but FUSE inbox has 2 items with legacy status="open" that the dispatcher silently skips. Total 79 / resolved 77 / open 2 pre-edit.
  Mode 3 — Defenses: NO PENDING surviving defenses.
  Mode 4 — Globe fingerprint: ALL DONE.
  Assigned issues (status==assigned-analyst): NONE.
  changes_pending_analysis: 0.

MODE 2b — OBE-TRIAGE on the two legacy-"open" inbox items (Phase 1 OBE limit 10/run, used 2):
  ATT-2026-04-20T14:54:53.038Z (WIN-019): ISS-1194 in closed-issues fixed_at 2026-04-20T14:55:08Z (10d ago); EXP-185 integrated 2026-04-20T14:41:38Z. Both gating refs satisfy >=3d OBE threshold. Resolved.
  ATT-2026-04-20T14:54:53.040Z (WIN-020): ISS-1204 in closed-issues fixed_at 2026-04-20T14:55:08Z (10d ago); EXP-186 integrated 2026-04-20T14:41:38Z. Both gating refs satisfy >=3d OBE threshold. Resolved.
  Both marked resolved_by=mode2b-obe-triage with resolved_reason explicitly noting the legacy status="open" normalization (so the audit trail explains why we touched them outside the strict pending-path).
  Post-edit FUSE inbox: 79 resolved / 0 open / 0 pending.

OTHER SIDE OF THE ARGUMENT (per operator user-preference):
  (1) "Should the dispatcher have caught these?" Counter: yes — Mode 2b's status==="pending" check is a de-facto coverage gap for any item written with a non-pending non-resolved label. These two were pushed by the decider 2026-04-20T14:54:53Z with status="open" (likely a transient schema state from when the decider was iterating on inbox semantics — note the timestamps are within ~15s of EXP-185/EXP-186 integration and ~15s of ISS-1194/1204 closure, so they were essentially DOA: the gating issues closed BEFORE these attention items were even pushed). They should probably never have been written. Surfacing this as a finding for tinker/decider review is the long-term fix; OBE-resolving on this run is the short-term cleanup.
  (2) "Is OBE-resolving outside the strict pending-path overstepping the dispatcher contract?" Counter: the OBE procedure (Mode 2b Phase 1) is mechanical and explicit — both items meet two independent OBE criteria (closed ISS >=3d AND integrated EXP >=3d). The resolved_reason fully documents the status-normalization rationale. Risk of leaving them: persistent invisible orphans that pollute future audits. Risk of resolving: essentially zero — they cannot meaningfully add scientific signal 10 days after their gating refs closed, and the inbox's scientific value is bounded by gating-ref freshness.
  (3) "Could status=="open" mean something semantically distinct from pending?" Counter: nothing in the dispatcher, the procedure docs, or the inbox's own past entries treats "open" as a third state — every other item is pending or resolved. The two anomalies are clustered at one timestamp pair, consistent with a one-off decider-side schema slip rather than a deliberate state.
  (4) "Should I have written an EXP/proposal for the verification rather than just OBE?" Counter: per Mode 2b, OBE is for items where the gating reference state has moved on — the substantive analysis was done by curmudgeon when ISS-1194/1204 were originally written, the decider patched the prose, and the EXPs were integrated. There is no science left to do; only bookkeeping. The Phase 3 singleton path is for items where re-examination is needed, which is not the case here.
